my_thoughts: They're mixing rage and anxiety. Teacher separates: rage is the REAL feeling about what happened, anxiety is what you feel when you defend against rage. This is signal vs defense distinction. Rage = appropriate response to abuse. Anxiety = body's way of blocking rage. Hypothesis: once they distinguish, they'll ask how to tell difference or what to do with rage. The distinction gives them diagnostic tool.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student conflates rage and anxiety - experiencing them as same. Teacher distinguishes: rage = primary feeling about abuse, anxiety = defense against rage. Uses comparison_contrast to separate signal (rage) from defense (anxiety). Predicting ask_deeper_question because distinctions typically produce questions about which is which.
visible_response: Your problem is not that you were enraged. Your problem was that, when you felt this enraged, it aroused tremendous anxiety.
